info.title: Global events QuakeML M3+ last 5 years
info.subtitle: Global M>=3 earthquake events QuakeML of last 5 years from 6 agencies
info.description:
This is a collection of global earthquake event QuakeML files gathered from 6 agencies (USGS, ISC, IRIS, EMSC, GFZ, IPGP) through their FDSN api using `obspy`, spanning last 5 years (sliding window, updated every month) with min magnitude of 3 or greater (note that due to ISC storing multiple magnitudes per event, their data will return any event with at least one magnitude greater or equal 3). 

**Disclaimer:** Please note that agencies don't update their data immediately. Usually it takes 1 to 2 years to fully gather and present to public data on smaller magnitudes. This dataset **cannot** be used as a valid representation of seismic activity in the most recent year or two, and is instead a way to archive data as it was published.

----------

Whole time period was split into chunks of 1 month. Exact time period is included in the name of subdir under `downloads`. Resulting QuakeML files were not altered in any way (note that ISC has formatting issue that adds `?` at the end of the file, which may cause QuakeML reader to fail).

Some agencies (ISC, USGS, IRIS, GFZ, IPGP) also got the `includeallorigins` and `includeallmagnitudes` flags so their QuakeML contain more data. EMSC did not implement this feature for case of requesting multiple events at once. Note that ISC in particular will include magnitude station contribution weights which add a lot of volume to the file.

-----------

Each folder contains `meta_query.json` which is a dump of parameters used for downloading events, plus some extra fields (like `data_format`, `label` and `time_window` that weren't passed to `obspy`). The `meta_last_downloads_report.json` contains a more detailed information over how download was performed, most importantly it has timestamps of beginning and end of entire download and each separate request.

------------

Created with: https://www.kaggle.com/code/thomasr25/global-events-quakeml-m3-last-5-years-many-agency (scheduled to update every month)

Using utility script, that implemented download wrapper over `obspy` library: https://www.kaggle.com/code/thomasr25/utilscript-quakes-download-and-analyse
